item.title: Intro to data visualization - R-Ladies Philly
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Philly

When: Wednesday, May 6, 2026 at 10:00 PM UTC

Where: Parkway Branch of the Free Library of Philadelphia

Attendees: 5 going

BYOLaptop and learn the grammar of graphics in R using ggplot2. Join us in person or on Zoom for a hybrid workshop, where you’ll explore the power of data visualization in R. Ama will lead the workshop with support from Shannon!

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Philly)

item.title: Intro to tidyverse - R-Ladies Philly
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Philly

When: Wednesday, April 29, 2026 at 10:00 PM UTC

Where: Parkway Branch of the Free Library of Philadelphia

Attendees: 6 going

BYOLaptop to learn data wrangling skills! Join us for a hybrid meeting either in-person or on zoom where participants will login to a virtual R session and learn to use Tidyverse packages and functions.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Philly)

item.title: Data Challenge Solution Showcase - R-Ladies Ottawa
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Ottawa

When: Tuesday, April 28, 2026 at 10:00 PM UTC

Where: Bayview Yards

Attendees: 5 going

**IWD Data Challenge 2026** For this year's data challenge, we asked you to explore and analyze data from your communities. Whether that meant digging into datasets about Ottawa, Gatineau, the broader National Capital Region, or another place that feels like home, the goal was to uncover community-focused insights. If you'd like more background on the challenge itself, you can find the full details in the [original event listing](https://www.meetup.com/rladies-ottawa/events/313489942/?utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=share-btn_savedevents_share_modal&utm_source=link&utm_version=v2&member_id=264488364) or on [our webpage](https://rladies-ottawa.github.io/events/2026/iwd.html). **Join Us for the Showcase Event** We're excited to celebrate the hard work and creativity of everyone who took part in this year's challenge. Join us for a fun, casual hybrid event on **Tuesday, April 28th**, where participants will walk us through their submissions. It's also a great oppotuity to connect with others in the city who are passionate about data, learning, and community! **Event Details** This event is **free to attend**, with limited in-person capacity. Please reserve an in-person spot only if you plan to join us onsite. Bayview Yards is located just a stone's throw away from the Bayview station. There is also a free parking lot for those who are driving. You can find us in the Training Room, located on the second floor of the building. **If You're Presenting** Please bring your laptop and charger so you can walk us through your project. **Community Guidelines** Please note that the mission of R-Ladies is to increase gender diversity in the R community. The data challenge and showcase are intended to provide a safe space for women and gender minorities. We ask for male allies to be invited by and accompanied by a woman or gender minority. **Special Thanks** We are grateful to be part of the Bayview Yards Meetup Program and extend our thanks to Bayview Yards for generously providing the venue space.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Ottawa)

item.title: Writing reports and articles with R in Quarto - R-Ladies Trondheim
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Trondheim

When: Tuesday, April 28, 2026 at 3:00 PM UTC

Where: Room ØHM, Øya helsehus

Attendees: 6 going

Quarto provides a convenient way to automatically create reports with results and figures generated from R-code. It can play an important role in a reproducible research workflow, and saves time since whenever the results change, the report is automatically up to date. It is a crucial tool in any R-lady's toolbox! Join us for an introductory tutorial in how to get the most use out of Quarto. You can find a map for the room here: https://link.mazemap.com/UKTLR7Tu

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Trondheim)

item.title: Career panel and networking - R-Ladies Philly
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Philly

When: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 10:00 PM UTC

Where: Parkway Branch of the Free Library of Philadelphia

Attendees: 6 going

Join us for a career panel and networking with established professionals who use R statistical programming language for data analysis in their career.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Philly)

item.title: Save the Date: Maps in R - R-Ladies St. Louis
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies St. Louis

When: Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 11:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 22 going

This will be a virtual event. Description is TBD. Topic is making maps in R.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies St. Louis)

item.title: ChatGPT in RStudio with gptstudio: Better than GitHub Copilot? - R-Ladies St. Louis
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies St. Louis

When: Friday, March 27, 2026 at 3:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 56 going

**Supercharging Your R Workflow with a Native AI Assistant** It’s no surprise that large language models like ChatGPT are helpful for coding. What makes gptstudio stand out is how it brings that help directly into RStudio through a clean, user-friendly add-in. gptstudio is an R package that integrates ChatGPT into RStudio, allowing you to generate code, explain functions, and write documentation using natural language, all without leaving your IDE. It supports various OpenAI models and offers an add-in panel that works like a built-in assistant. This tool is especially useful when you're experimenting, writing boilerplate code, or just need quick answers while staying in your flow. Advantages of using the gptstudio package: ✔️ Seamless integration into RStudio via an add-in panel ✔️ Helps generate and explain R code using plain language prompts ✔️ Supports multiple OpenAI models, including GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 ✔️ Useful for both beginners and experienced users looking to speed up their workflow

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies St. Louis)

item.title: Análisis de datos que visibilizan las exigencias y luchas de las mujeres - R-Ladies Guadalajara
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Guadalajara

When: Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 1:00 AM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 20 going

¿Te has preguntado qué datos existen actualmente sobre la violencia contra las mujeres? ¿Hay evidencia de estas exigencias? ¿Quiénes producen y miden estos datos, y por qué? ¿Qué registros se generan y cuáles son sus limitaciones? ¿Qué papel pueden tener los datos para visibilizar las luchas y demandas de las mujeres? En este taller teórico-práctico exploraremos los principales indicadores sobre violencia contra niñas, adolescentes y mujeres. Analizaremos qué dicen las cifras, cómo interpretar tendencias y cuáles son los alcances y límites de los datos disponibles. Además, reflexionaremos sobre cómo comunicar esta información para dimensionar las violencias y colocarlas en la conversación pública. 25 marzo 2026 19:00 HRS (CDMX) Vía meet Cupo limitado ***R-Ladies Guadalajara promueve espacios seguros y respetuosos. Este evento se rige por nuestro código de conducta, que busca garantizar una participación libre de violencias, discriminación o acoso.*** ###

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Guadalajara)

item.title: Instalación de paquetes en R sin morir en el intento - R-Ladies Cuernavaca
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Cuernavaca

When: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 11:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 17 going

En este meetup daremos un vistazo general al proceso para instalar R y RStudio en diferentes sistemas operativos, asi como también la instalación de algunos paquetes a través de repositorios CRAN, Bioconductor, Github,Conda y paquetes locales. Revisaremos los posibles problemas que nos podremos encontrar al realizar estas instalaciones. # Requerimientos de software * Descarga el paquete de R segun tu sistema operativo desde: https://cran.itam.mx/ * Descarga el paquete de RStudio segun tu sistema operativo desde: https://posit.co/downloads/

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Cuernavaca)

item.title: Level Up Your Data Visualizations with ggplot2 - R-Ladies Abuja
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Abuja

When: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 4:00 PM UTC

Attendees: 36 going

Join us for a practical, beginner-friendly meetup on **“Level Up Your Data Visualizations with ggplot2”** where we’ll explore how to create clear, compelling, and professional charts in R using **ggplot2**. Whether you’re new to data visualization or looking to improve your plotting skills, this session will cover key principles, useful customization tips, and hands-on examples to help you turn raw data into impactful visual stories.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Abuja)

item.title: Working Smarter with {dplyr} 1.2.0 – A Practical Session - R-Ladies Rome
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Rome

When: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 5:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 72 going

**{dplyr} 1.2.0 is out, and wow, is it a delight!** **Join us for a tour** of the new tools in {dplyr} 1.2.0 and when you should use them. We will walk through **WHEN** you should use **ANY** of the new functionality (there are new \`***when_any()***\` and \`***when_all()***\` functions), how to say goodbye to case_match() and what to **REPLACE** it with, and more. You won't want to \`filter_out()\` this release. **Join us to learn more!** The tidyverse keeps evolving, and **{dplyr} 1.2.0** brings a set of new tools that make data manipulation even more expressive and flexible. Whether you regularly work with **R and the tidyverse**, or you’re looking to sharpen your data wrangling skills, this session will help you better understand the design philosophy behind the latest **{dplyr}** release and how to apply it effectively. Expect a clear walkthrough, practical examples, and time for questions.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Rome)

item.title: Data driven decision making for fantasy basketball - R-Ladies Melbourne
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Melbourne

When: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 6:30 AM UTC

Where: DiUS

Attendees: 30 going

Integral to the modern data scientists toolkit is understanding how to turn data into decisions. A core part of this is thinking about the end stage of your analysis pipeline, and how the communication and visualisation of your results will factor into decision making. But it's not just enough to deliver a solution. Part of successfully supporting end-user decisions comes from co-design. That means thinking about end user needs and how your end-users will engage with your solution. This talk explores both of these issues using fantasy basketball statistics. So come along if you have a love of data and data-driven decision making. Also come along if you are fan of sports analytics or forecasting.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Melbourne)
